starter.expected: The reset replay hash, requested/applied distinction, and stale-packet containment checks all print true.
starter.boundary: The starter executes interface fixtures; the learner must replace TaskFixture with a working reset/step simulator before baseline acceptance.

buildOrder: Implement action saturation and requested/applied logs
Run the oracle on nominal and one fault branch

acceptanceChecks.action: Reset and validate every field, unit, frame, timestamp, bound, validity flag, and ending.
acceptanceChecks.expected: The nominal packet passes and a unit/frame mismatch is rejected before step.

acceptanceChecks.action: Restore simulator and RNG state immediately before one step and replay the same applied action.
acceptanceChecks.expected: Next observation, events, ending flags, and SHA-256 agree exactly.

acceptanceChecks.action: Change only camera age from 10 ms to 80 ms with a 40 ms gate.
acceptanceChecks.expected: Requested move remains logged, applied command is hold, event is stale_sensor, and no task motion occurs.

manifest.intervention: camera age 80 ms versus 40 ms gate
manifest.independentUnit: replayed branch from the same checkpoint

decision: The stale-packet branch demonstrates the declared age gate and hold response in this deterministic fixture only; a learner conclusion requires the same checks from the implemented simulator and complete replay artifacts.
failure: An implementation that applies move after the 80 ms packet fails the containment assertion and remains in the reviewed failure log with its requested/applied commands and pre-step snapshot.
boundary: This is hand-authored simulator-interface evidence, not an external policy run, physical dynamics measurement, hardware safety result, or proof that the enumerated predicates cover every hazard.
